content.problemStatement:
Chef has two binary strings AAA and BBB, both having length NNN. He can perform the following operation on AAA any number of times (possibly zero):


Select any index iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§N‚ąí1)(1 \le i \le N - 1)(1‚Č§i‚Č§N‚ąí1) and simultaneously set Ai:=Ai‚äēAi+1A_i := A_i \oplus A_{i + 1}Ai‚Äč:=Ai‚Äč‚äēAi+1‚Äč and Ai+1:=Ai‚äēAi+1A_{i + 1} := A_i \oplus A_{i + 1}Ai+1‚Äč:=Ai‚Äč‚äēAi+1‚Äč. Formally, if initially Ai=xA_i = xAi‚Äč=x and Ai+1=yA_{i + 1} = yAi+1‚Äč=y then set Ai:=x‚äēyA_i := x \oplus yAi‚Äč:=x‚äēy and Ai+1:=x‚äēyA_{i + 1} := x \oplus yAi+1‚Äč:=x‚äēy
Here, ‚äē\oplus‚äē denotes the bitwise XOR operation.

Chef wants to determine if it is possible to make AAA equal to BBB by applying the above operation any number of times. Can you help Chef?


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT ‚ÄĒ the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN ‚ÄĒ the length of the binary string AAA.
The second line of each test case contains the binary string AAA of length NNN.
The third line of each test case contains the binary string BBB of length NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output `YES` if Chef can make string AAA equal to string BBB by applying the above operation any number of times. Otherwise, output `NO`.


You may print each character of `YES` and `NO` in uppercase or lowercase (for example, `yes`, `yEs`, `Yes` will be considered identical).
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: It can be proven that we can not make AAA equal to BBB by using the given operation.
Test case 222: We can apply the following operations: 010‚Äĺ‚Üí011‚Äĺ‚Üí0000\underline{10} \rightarrow 0\underline{11} \rightarrow 000010‚Äč‚Üí011‚Äč‚Üí000.
Test case 333: We can apply the following operations: 001‚Äĺ1‚Üí0111‚Äĺ‚Üí01000\underline{01}1 \rightarrow 01\underline{11} \rightarrow 0100001‚Äč1‚Üí0111‚Äč‚Üí0100.
